mission.title: cri-o: Add support for dual stack IPv6
mission.description:
We have to vendor the latest Kubernetes master as well as OCICNI to get
the latest support for dual-stack IPv6. This adds an `AdditionalIps`
field to the network status, which will be internally handled as a
simple slice of strings. This means that the pod annotations now can
contain multiple IPs as well. The same applied to the inspect HTTP API.

Relates to: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/73977
Current status: Under testing since the kubernetes PR has been merged.

steps.title: Only the first two cidrs are used (soft limits for Alpha, might be lifted lat...
steps.description: Only the first two cidrs are used (soft limits for Alpha, might be lifted later on).

steps.title: Only the "RangeAllocator" (default) is allowed as a value for --cidr-allocato...
steps.description: Only the "RangeAllocator" (default) is allowed as a value for --cidr-allocator-type . Cloud allocators are not compatible with ipv6dualstack

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
CC @lachie83 @thockin


### What is in the box? (alpha status)
- Node ipam controller now supports multi-cidr per node (node.Spec.PodCIDRs).
- Pod.PodStatus.PodIPs supports multiple IPs.
- Route Controller now supports creating routes `per node's cidr`.
- kubenet now supports dualstack. 
- Azure support `Route() interface` implementation for dualstack.
- Known issue: kubenet will always force reporting ipv4, ipv6 (irrespective of podCIDRs order).
- updates to CRI to support reporting m
```

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/73977. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

CC @lachie83 @thockin


### What is in the box? (alpha status)
- Node ipam controller now supports multi-cidr per node (node.Spec.PodCIDRs).
- Pod.PodStatus.PodIPs supports multiple IPs.
- Route Controller now supports creating routes `per node's cidr`.

CC @lachie83 @thockin ### What is in the box? (alpha status) - Node ipam controller now supports multi-cidr per node (node.Spec.PodCIDRs). - Pod.PodStatus.PodIPs supports multiple IPs. - Route Controller now supports creating routes `per node's cidr`. - kubenet now supports dualstack. - Azure support `Route() interface` implementation for dualstack. - Known issue: kubenet will always force reporting ipv4, ipv6 (irrespective of podCIDRs order). - updates to CRI to support reporting multiple IPs per `PodSandBox`. ### Known Issues - Cluster ipv6 CIDRS mask bigger than `24` will fail - ipv6 cidr assignment is using the default ipv4 cidr `/24` (Future: add controls over v6 cidr size) - kubenet forces `v4,v6` reporting of IPs, users who must`v6,v4` as `--cluster-cidr` - Masquerading is not done by kubenet. Users will have to use `ip-masq-agent` to perform masquerading correctly for ipv6. prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cri-o installed or the issue environment reproducible.
